As an AI language model, I am not sure which sentences need to be revised as I cannot see the context. Could you please provide me with the context or specific sentences you would like me to revise?
A donor DNA that contains the insert or mutation sequence flanked by two arm sequences derived from the chromosomal target site were PCR amplified from the S. islandicus genomic DNA.
The donor DNA containing the mutation sequence enclosed by two arm sequences derived from the chromosomal target site was PCR amplified from S. islandicus genomic DNA.
The donor DNA consists of the inserted or mutated sequence flanked by two arm sequences derived from the chromosomal target site, and the arm sequence is amplified from S. islandicus genomic DNA.

This revised sentence maintains the original meaning, but improves the grammar:

"The donor DNA, which comprises the inserted or mutated sequence flanked by two arm sequences derived from the chromosomal target site, was PCR amplified from S. islandicus genomic DNA."

The primers used in this study were obtained from Sangon (Shanghai). For gene editing purposes, we employed a pSe-Rp vector, which includes a sequence cassette that can be inserted into a specific site of interest through homology recombination, as well as a mini-CRISPR array that targets the wild-type insertion site (Li et al., 2016). A spacer sequence designed to target the original insertion site was chemically synthesized and inserted into the BspMI sites of pSe-Rp through restriction cleavage and ligation using T4 DNA ligase. The donor DNA, which comprises the inserted or mutated sequence flanked by two arm sequences derived from the chromosomal target site, was PCR amplified from S. islandicus genomic DNA. Vector was linearized through cleavage at the SalI and NotI sites and then ligated with the donor DNA using the In-fusion method. The resulting plasmid was transformed into S. islandicus competent cells by electroporation

The primers used in this study were obtained from Sangon (Shanghai). To edit the gene, we utilized a pSe-Rp vector that has a sequence cassette, which can be inserted into a particular site of interest via homology recombination, along with a mini-CRISPR array that targets the wild-type insertion site (Li et al., 2016). We designed a spacer sequence chemically and inserted it into the BspMI sites of pSe-Rp using restriction cleavage and ligation by T4 DNA ligase to target the initial insertion site. The donor DNA, comprising the inserted or mutated sequence flanked by two arm sequences derived from the chromosomal target site, was amplified from S. islandicus genomic DNA via PCR. After cleavage at the SalI and NotI sites, the vector was linearized and then ligated with the donor DNA using the In-fusion method. We subsequently transformed the resulting plasmid into S. islandicus proficient cells through electroporation.

Subsequently, the transformed cells were plated on medium lacking uracil, and clones were selected by a PCR-based assay. By this way, a 3×Flag and 6×His epitope tag was inserted into the C-terminus of chromosomal Cbf5 gene and individual genes of sR109, sR148 and sR563 were removed.

After transformation, the cells were plated on uracil-deficient medium, and we used a PCR-based assay to select clones. Subsequently, a 3×Flag and 6×His epitope tag was inserted at the C-terminus of the chromosomal Cbf5 gene and individual genes of sR109, sR148, and sR563 were removed.
Subsequently, the transformed cells were plated on medium lacking uracil, and clones were selected by a PCR-based assay and sequencing.
After transformation, the cells were plated on uracil-deficient medium, and clones were selected using a PCR-based assay and sequencing.

Cells expressing Cbf5-FH were harvested and frozen in liquid nitrogen. They were then subjected to grinding using a Tissue grinder (Tissuelyser-24) at 70 Hz for ten times in buffer S (100mM MOPS pH 7.4, 300mM NaCl and 10% glycerol), supplemented with protease inhibitor tablets (Roche). Following grinding, an equal volume of lysis buffer (0.2% NP40, 100mM MOPS pH 7.4, 300mM NaCl and 10% glycerol) was added to the lysate, which was then incubated on ice for 30 min with intermittent mixing. After removal of cell debris by centrifugation at 30,000×g, the supernatant was incubated with 600 μL of anti-FLAG M2 affinity beads (Sigma) at 4°C with rotation for 1 hour. The beads were subsequently washed with buffer S until the OD280 of the wash solution dropped below 0.05. The protein was eluted twice with 900 μL of 150 μg/ml 3×FLAG peptide (Sigma). The combined eluate was bound to 50 μL of Ni-NTA beads (Promega, V8560) in buffer S. The beads were washed 3 times with buffer S containing 20 mM imidazole and eluted in 100 μL of buffer S containing 500 mM imidazole. For further purification, the IP RNPs were separated by a 5% Tris-Glycine native gel and strained by SYBR Gold (Invitrogen, S11494). The bands were excised and RNPs were extracted by crush and soak.

The following is a revised version of the paragraph with improved wording and grammar: 

To prepare cells expressing Cbf5-FH for analysis, they were frozen in liquid nitrogen and then ground using a Tissue grinder (Tissuelyser-24) ten times at 70 Hz in buffer S (100mM MOPS pH 7.4, 300mM NaCl, and 10% glycerol) containing protease inhibitor tablets (Roche). After grinding, an equal volume of lysis buffer (0.2% NP40, 100mM MOPS pH 7.4, 300mM NaCl, and 10% glycerol) was added to the lysate, which was left to incubate on ice for 30 minutes with intermittent mixing. Following centrifugation at 30,000×g to remove cell debris, the supernatant was incubated with 600 μL of anti-FLAG M2 affinity beads (Sigma) at 4°C with rotation for one hour. The beads were then washed with buffer S until the OD280 of the wash solution dropped below 0.05. The protein was eluted twice with 900 μL of 150 μg/ml 3×FLAG peptide (Sigma). The combined eluate was bound to 50 μL of Ni-NTA beads (Promega, V8560) in buffer S. The beads were washed three times with buffer S containing 20 mM imidazole and eluted in 100 μL of buffer S containing 500 mM imidazole. To further purify the IP RNPs, they were separated by a 5% Tris-Glycine native gel and stained with SYBR Gold (Invitrogen, S11494). After excising the bands, the RNPs were extracted by crush and soak.
The bands were excised and RNPs were extracted by from the gel in RNA elution buffer (300 mM sodium acetate pH 5.5, 1 mM EDTA, 0.25% SDS and 100U/mL RNasin (Promega; N2615)).

The revised sentence below improves the grammar and adds clarity:

"Following separation by a 5% Tris-Glycine native gel and staining with SYBR Gold (Invitrogen, S11494), the bands were excised, and RNPs were extracted from the gel using RNA elution buffer (300 mM sodium acetate pH 5.5, 1 mM EDTA, 0.25% SDS, and 100U/mL RNasin (Promega; N2615))."
The IP and gel-purified RNPs were subjected to RNA-seq and mass spectrometry analysis. Mass spectrometry analysis was conducted as previously described.
We performed RNA-seq and mass spectrometry analysis on both the IP and gel-purified RNPs. The mass spectrometry analysis was conducted using the methods described previously.

The bands were excised and RNAs were extracted from the gel slices in RNA elution buffer (300 mM sodium acetate pH 5.5, 1 mM EDTA, 0.25% SDS and 100U/mL RNasin (Promega; N2615)) overnight at 22°C with gentle rocking. RNA was added with 1μL of GlycoBlue (Invitrogen, AM9515) and an equal volume of isopropanol, incubated at -80°C for 3 hour and precipitated by centrifugation at 15,000 rpm and 4°C for 30 min. The pelleted RNA was washed twice with 75% ethanol, dried on air and resuspended in diethylpyrocarbonate (DEPC)-treated water.

We extracted RNAs from the gel slices by excising the bands and using RNA elution buffer (300 mM sodium acetate pH 5.5, 1 mM EDTA, 0.25% SDS, and 100U/mL RNasin (Promega; N2615)) overnight at 22°C with gentle rocking. We added 1 μL of GlycoBlue (Invitrogen, AM9515) and an equal volume of isopropanol to the RNA, incubated them at -80°C for three hours, and precipitated them by centrifugation at 15,000 rpm and 4°C for 30 minutes. After washing twice with 75% ethanol, the pelleted RNA was dried in air and resuspended in diethylpyrocarbonate (DEPC)-treated water.

RNA was dephosphorylated with Quick CIP (NEB; M0525S) and T4 polynucleotide kinase (PNK) (NEB; M0201S), precipitated with isopropanol and resuspended. RNA was 5' phosphorylated with T4 PNK and extracted with phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1, pH 5.2). RNA sequencing libraries were constructed with NEBNext Multiplex Small RNA Library Prep Set for Illumina (E7300S, NEB) using our adaptors and primers (Wu 2020).

We used Quick CIP (NEB; M0525S) and T4 polynucleotide kinase (PNK) (NEB; M0201S) to dephosphorylate RNA, which was then precipitated with isopropanol and further resuspended. Afterwards, RNA was 5' phosphorylated with T4 PNK, and we extracted it with phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1, pH 5.2). We constructed RNA sequencing libraries using our adaptors and primers (Wu 2020) and the NEBNext Multiplex Small RNA Library Prep Set for Illumina (E7300S, NEB).

RNA was dephosphorylated with Quick CIP (NEB; M0525S) and T4 polynucleotide kinase (PNK) (NEB; M0201S), then precipitated with isopropanol and further resuspended. RNA was 5' phosphorylated with T4 PNK, and we extracted it with phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1, pH 5.2). We constructed RNA sequencing libraries using our adaptors and primers (Wu 2020) and the NEBNext Multiplex Small RNA Library Prep Set for Illumina (E7300S, NEB).
This revised sentence is identical to the previous response. No further changes are necessary.

Ψ-seq was conducted using the previously described and slightly modified protocol (Schwartz et al., 2014). Total RNA (15 μg in 25 μL) was mixed with 25 μL of 2× alkaline hydrolysis buffer (90 mM NaHCO3, 10 mM Na2CO3, pH 9.2) and fragmented at 95°C for 10 min. RNA was precipitated with isopropanol and dissolved in DEPC-treated water.

We used the slightly modified protocol (Schwartz et al., 2014) to conduct Ψ-seq. Initially, we mixed total RNA (15 μg in 25 μL) with 25 μL of 2× alkaline hydrolysis buffer (90 mM NaHCO3, 10 mM Na2CO3, pH 9.2) and fragmented them at 95°C for 10 minutes. Subsequently, we precipitated RNA with isopropanol and dissolved them in DEPC-treated water.

We used the slightly modified protocol (Schwartz et al., 2014) to conduct Ψ-seq. Total RNA (15 μg in 25 μL) was mixed with 25 μL of 2× alkaline hydrolysis buffer (90 mM NaHCO3, 10 mM Na2CO3, pH 9.2) and fragmented at 95°C for 10 min. RNA was precipitated with isopropanol and dissolved in DEPC-treated water. Three fifths of RNA was dissolved in BEU buffer (50 mM bicine pH 8.5, 4mM EDTA, 7 M urea) containing 0.2 M N-cyclohexyl-N’-(2-morpholinoethyl) carbodiimide methyl-p-toluenesulfonate (CMC, Sigma) and incubated at 37°C for 40 min. Two fifths of RNA was dissolved in BEU buffer without CMC. Both RNA samples were precipitated with ethanol, resuspended in Na2CO3 buffer (50 mM sodium carbonate pH 10.4, 2 mM EDTA) and incubated at 45°C for 2 h, followed by ethanol precipitation. RNA of length 80-150nt was separated by 8% polyacrylamide urea gel for library construction.
To prepare sRNAs, total RNA was separated by 8% urea polyacrylamide gel and 20 to 400 nt RNAs were extracted from gel in RNA elution buffer (300 mM sodium acetate pH 5.5, 1 mM EDTA, 0.25% SDS and 100U/mL RNasin (Promega; N2615)).

We used the modified protocol (Schwartz et al., 2014) to conduct Ψ-seq. Initially, we mixed total RNA (15 μg in 25 μL) with 25 μL of 2× alkaline hydrolysis buffer (90 mM NaHCO3, 10 mM Na2CO3, pH 9.2) and fragmented them at 95°C for 10 minutes. We then precipitated RNA with isopropanol and dissolved them in DEPC-treated water. 

For Ψ-seq, three-fifths of the RNA was dissolved in BEU buffer (50 mM bicine pH 8.5, 4mM EDTA, 7 M urea) containing 0.2 M N-cyclohexyl-N’-(2-morpholinoethyl) carbodiimide methyl-p-toluenesulfonate (CMC, Sigma) and incubated at 37°C for 40 minutes. Two-fifths of the RNA was dissolved in BEU buffer without CMC. Both RNA samples were precipitated with ethanol, resuspended in Na2CO3 buffer (50 mM sodium carbonate pH 10.4, 2 mM EDTA), and incubated at 45°C for two hours before additional ethanol precipitation. RNA of length 80-150 nt was separated by 8% polyacrylamide urea gel for library construction.

To prepare sRNAs, we separated total RNA using an 8% urea polyacrylamide gel, and then we extracted RNAs ranging from 20-400 nt in size from the gel using RNA elution buffer (300 mM sodium acetate pH 5.5, 1 mM EDTA, 0.25% SDS, and 100U/mL RNasin (Promega; N2615)).
